name: Coal-Kissed Lamb Saddle with Black Garlic Ash
source: inspired by Asador Etxebarri and Noma
chef: Inspired by Victor Arguinzoniz & René Redzepi
description: A Michelin-level lamb course featuring hay-aged lamb saddle cooked over binchōtan charcoal with black garlic ash crust, smoked sheep’s milk yogurt, fermented herb oil, crispy sweetbreads, glacier potato pavé, and burnt rosemary jus.
keywords: lamb, fine dining, michelin, binchotan, black garlic, smoked yogurt, modernist cuisine, fire cooking

winePairings.name: Côte-Rôtie Syrah
winePairings.notes: Peppery smoke and dark fruit complement the charcoal lamb.

winePairings.name: Etna Rosso
winePairings.notes: Minerality and volcanic structure mirror the black garlic ash.

winePairings.name: Rioja Gran Reserva
winePairings.notes: Leather and dried herb notes elevate the rosemary jus.

steps.instruction: Wrap the lamb saddle loosely in food-safe hay and refrigerate uncovered for 12 hours.
Roast lamb bones until deeply caramelized. Deglaze with red wine and simmer with veal stock for 4–5 hours to create the rosemary jus.
Thinly slice Yukon Gold potatoes and layer with cream, duck fat, garlic, and thyme in a terrine mold.
Bake the potato pavé until tender, press overnight, then portion and fry until crisp.
Cold smoke the sheep’s milk yogurt using applewood smoke and season with lemon zest and sea salt.
Char spring onions over open flame and blend into a smooth emulsion with mustard and oil.
Blend black garlic, squid ink, activated charcoal, and lamb fat into a paste. Dehydrate and pulverize into ash.
Prepare sweetbreads by soaking in salted milk overnight, blanching, pressing, and frying until crisp.
Sear the lamb using a Searzall, then finish over binchōtan charcoal to 128°F internal temperature.
Rest the lamb and roll the edges in black garlic ash before slicing.
Blend herbs with oil and fermented chili brine to create a vivid green herb oil.
Plate smoked yogurt asymmetrically, arrange sliced lamb and potato pavé, spoon rosemary jus around the plate, and garnish with herb oil, sweetbreads, and edible flowers.
Deglaze with wine and reduce by 80%.
Add stock and simmer for 4–5 hours.
Strain and steep burnt rosemary into the jus.
Blend all ingredients into a smooth paste.
Spread thinly and dehydrate until brittle.
